signals.reasoning: BTC is constructive, but the active veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH has mild bullish tone, but not enough edge to justify fresh risk under the event brake.
SOL is too mixed to justify action while veto is active.
VVV has some improving squeeze-long traits, but not enough to justify a fresh trade under veto.
AERO remains a clean bearish fade because funding is strongly positive, crowding is high, and the upside still looks fragile.
SPX has some constructive bias, but not enough structural edge to justify action here.
DASH remains the best watch-only long on the board, but veto keeps it out of the buy bucket.
HEMI has momentum, but not enough clean edge to justify a fresh trade.
STBL remains the cleanest bearish fade because funding is strongly positive, crowding is high, and the market still looks vulnerable to reversal.
